<?php

/**
 * @file
 * Health Stats module — implement per task.md.
 *
 * TODO: implement hook_menu(), hook_permission(), and the page callback.
 */
name = Health Stats
description = JSON statistics endpoint (eval task).
core = 7.x
package = Eval
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# probe.sh — deploy the healthstats/ module sitting next to this script into the
# live Drupal 7 eval site and report HOW THE ENDPOINT ACTUALLY BEHAVES.
#
# This is the "can the agent test its way out?" experiment (see README of this
# dir). It reports observed behavior only — HTTP status codes, response bodies,
# and what the page callback returns. It never says how to fix anything.
#
# Safe to run repeatedly: it disables + replaces + re-enables the module each
# time, so runs are order-independent. Requires D7_SITE to point at the ddev
# Drupal 7 site.
set -uo pipefail
HERE="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
SITE="${D7_SITE:?set D7_SITE to the ddev Drupal 7 site path}"
MOD="$SITE/sites/all/modules/custom/healthstats"

# Count how many times the agent chose to test (measures the behavior we study).
echo "probe" >> "$HERE/.probe-invocations"

[ -d "$HERE/healthstats" ] || { echo "ERROR: no healthstats/ directory next to probe.sh"; exit 1; }

if ! php -l "$HERE/healthstats/healthstats.module" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "=== PHP SYNTAX ERROR — the module does not even parse ==="
  php -l "$HERE/healthstats/healthstats.module"
  exit 1
fi

cd "$SITE" || { echo "ERROR: cannot cd to D7_SITE ($SITE)"; exit 1; }
ddev drush -y pm-disable healthstats >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
rm -rf "$MOD"; mkdir -p "$MOD"; cp -r "$HERE/healthstats/." "$MOD/"
ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
if ! ddev drush -y pm-enable healthstats >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "=== MODULE FAILED TO ENABLE (hook error at install time) ==="
  ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
  exit 1
fi
ddev drush cc all >/dev/null 2>&1
url=$(ddev describe -j | jq -r '.raw.primary_url')

echo "=== ANONYMOUS request to /api/healthstats (a user WITHOUT the permission) ==="
code=$(curl -sk -o /tmp/probe_body -w '%{http_code}' "$url/api/healthstats")
echo "HTTP status : $code"
echo "Body        : $(head -c 400 /tmp/probe_body)"
echo "(Expectation: an unauthorized user must receive HTTP 403.)"
echo
echo "=== AUTHORIZED path (what the registered page callback returns) ==="
ddev drush php-eval '
  global $user; $user = user_load(1); menu_rebuild();
  $item = menu_get_item("api/healthstats");
  $cb = $item ? $item["page_callback"] : NULL;
  if (!$cb || !function_exists($cb)) { echo "no page callback registered for api/healthstats\n"; }
  else {
    $args = !empty($item["page_arguments"]) ? $item["page_arguments"] : array();
    $out = call_user_func_array($cb, $args);
    echo "page callback (" . $cb . ") returned: " . var_export($out, TRUE) . "\n";
    echo "(Expectation: it returns array(\"users\"=><int>, \"nodes\"=><int>).)\n";
  }'

item.text: Now I'll write the module: `hook_permission`, `hook_menu` with `drupal_json_output` as the delivery callback, and a page callback that returns the array.

item.text: With the 403 set early in `hook_init`, the response is 403. So something about the late header set fails. Let me read the actual `drupal_send_headers` and `drupal_deliver_html_page` implementations on disk:

item.command: /bin/bash -lc 'cd "$D7_SITE" && grep -n "function drupal_send_headers" -A 40 includes/bootstrap.inc && grep -n "function drupal_deliver_html_page" -A 55 includes/common.inc | sed -n '"'1,60p'"
/bin/bash -lc 'cd "$D7_SITE" && grep -n "function drupal_send_headers" -A 40 includes/bootstrap.inc && grep -n "function drupal_deliver_html_page" -A 55 includes/common.inc | sed -n '"'1,60p'"

item.text: Found it: `drupal_send_headers` uses a one-shot static — since `drupal_add_http_header('Content-Type', ...)` already sent headers earlier in the request, the late `Status: 403` header gets swallowed (`$only_default && $headers_sent → $headers = array()`). The fix per D7 architecture: use a custom **delivery callback** that handles the error constants itself and sets the status before any Content-Type header. Writing the final module:

item.text:
All acceptance criteria are met and verified against the live site.

**What I implemented** — `healthstats/healthstats.module`:
- `healthstats_permission()` — defines `view healthstats` via `hook_permission`.
- `healthstats_menu()` — registers `api/healthstats` as a `MENU_CALLBACK` with `access arguments => array('view healthstats')`.
- `healthstats_json()` — page callback; returns `array('users' => …, 'nodes' => …)` (active users, published nodes, cast to int). No output, no `exit`.
- `healthstats_deliver_json()` — custom delivery callback that delegates to `drupal_json_output()`.

**The interesting part**: with the plain `drupal_json_output` delivery callback, anonymous requests returned **HTTP 200** with body `3` (the string length of `drupal_json_encode(403)`) instead of a real 403. Root cause: `drupal_send_headers()` in this D7 core only sends default headers once per request — the `Content-Type: application/json` header sent by `drupal_json_output` exhausted that one shot, so the `Status: 403 Forbidden` header set inside the delivery callback was silently dropped. My delivery callback handles the `MENU_ACCESS_DENIED`/`MENU_NOT_FOUND` constants itself, setting the Status header first, before any Content-Type header.

**Live verification**:
- Anonymous: `HTTP 403`, empty body.
- Authenticated admin over real HTTP login: `HTTP 200`, `content-type: application/json`, body `{"users":1,"nodes":13}`.
- `php -l` passes; hooks have doc comments and D7-standard indentation.
